title: Existentialism and Eva
wikitext:
[]: Existentialism and Eva...             Quote Post by Phaze » Thu Jul 01, 2004 4:21 pm I read a little bit on existentialism and noticed many interesting (though perhaps unintentional parallels) such as: Humanitys will to live, proof of mankinds existence, creating your own reality, and many other basic themes. It would be nice if somone who actually has some knowledge could go deeper into this, or just share their thoughts about what these existing parallels could mean.

[]:             Quote Post by Somnius Kenate » Mon Jul 05, 2004 3:43 pm Evangelion was manily created to tap into the mind (cliched, or stereotyped) of the socialy isolated otaku. Let's face it...people who watch Evangelion, or anime in general, are not usually not most popular people when they were in high school. I for one am perfectly fine by that. Existentialism emphasizes isolation in a hostile world as a positive experience. Shinji believes that if he does not have relationships with people, he will not end up hurting them. Social isolation does little to expand the interaction with others and because of the lack of relationships, creates a world that is nothing but yourself, and that can get quite lonely. But in a hostile world, everyone would just want to curl up in a little ball and be left alone. Hideaki Anno created this anime because he wanted it to attract a fickle anime fanbase (cute girls, giant fighting robots, depressed main male character) and build on the cliches that were used. But with that idea, he expanded and made the character step out of that world. Sure, everyone goes through pain, but you either let that destroy you, or learn from your mistakes. Anno hoped that if you realize that if you understand pain, and realize that this is a pessimistic world, you would understand that happiness is attainable if you create it in your own mind. Sorry if I went a little off-topic.

[]:             Quote Post by max3000 » Mon Jul 05, 2004 4:39 pm Somnius Kenate wrote: Evangelion was manily created to tap into the mind (cliched, or stereotyped) of the socialy isolated otaku. Let's face it...people who watch Evangelion, or anime in general, are not usually not most popular people when they were in high school. I for one am perfectly fine by that.

[]:             Quote Post by Somnius Kenate » Mon Jul 05, 2004 4:52 pm I didn't want to sound rude, I was just saying that the more technologically-refined people are not usually looked highly upon by the ignorant jocks and preps.

[]:             Quote Post by max3000 » Mon Jul 05, 2004 5:07 pm Somnius Kenate wrote: I didn't want to sound rude, I was just saying that the more technologically-refined people are not usually looked highly upon by the ignorant jocks and preps.

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Mon Jul 05, 2004 5:20 pm max3000 wrote: Actually, i was like that two years ago, when i moved to a different town. I was quite shy and not very social. But i met new people and friends and sinc then i changed. I started noticing that i was doing stuff that i would never even thought about doing.

[]:             Quote Post by max3000 » Mon Jul 05, 2004 5:31 pm I try to be as different from Shinji as i can be. IMO Anno intended NGE to set an example of what NOT to do.

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Mon Jul 05, 2004 5:39 pm max3000 wrote: I try to be as different from Shinji as i can be. IMO Anno intended NGE to set an example of what NOT to do.

[]:             Quote Post by Somnius Kenate » Tue Jul 06, 2004 12:13 am I personally think that the Evangelion itself is a symbol for what we think is our reason for being in this earth. "I am a student; a lawyer; a wife; a singer, etc." We place our reason for being her on material things that could be taken away at ay moment. Shinji thought his only reason for being here was to pilot Eva. So did Asuka and Rei. They had nothing else. But once the angels were defeated, they had nothing left. They had to find their own happiness.

[]:             Quote Post by chocomanji » Fri Jul 09, 2004 11:11 pm Hmmm....once being an expert on Existentialism, I can see plenty of parallels. As Somnius stated, one of the most expressed aspects of Existentialism is individual purpose. For example, existentialist author Albert Camus' "The Stranger" showed a character who did things simply because that's what you were suppose to do: like when he got engaged not because he really loved a woman, but b/c you were socially obligated to marry. Not to go into too much detail, this character accepts his fate in the end b/c "it was meant to be" his own personal choices led him to where he was destined. Here, Shinji, does not exactly want to be an Eva pilot, but feels that it's his only real purpose in life. In the end, it is his own choice and decision of what he wants that instrumentality becomes. Then you see Rei, and her three selves. Rei I and II practically know nothing about why they exist, reflexing the Existentialist aspect that we do not know what our purpose is initially, but we do choose what happens with our actions, whether we know it or not. She becomes in a way, a puppet to Gendo while she questions her own existance. When Rei III finally discovers what's really going on, she decides on her own what step she must take next, initiating 3rd Impact when she fuses with Lillith. She independently realizes what her purpose is and acts upon it. Okay...well, I'll return to this topic later...I have a lot to say, but I don't have the time right now to logically organize it... :shock:

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Sat Jul 10, 2004 5:58 pm i think anno is trying to say that running away from reality will only hurt you more, as when Shinji feels all the other people's feelings while instrumentality is still kinda complete (the truth is painful but being ambiguous only causes more pain) perhaps he is trying to tell up to face reality and accept it no matter how much it sucks...this probably has to do with his depression during the making of the episodes...and maybe even the movie...

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Sun Jul 11, 2004 2:30 am sadsadshinji wrote: i think anno is trying to say that running away from reality will only hurt you more, as when Shinji feels all the other people's feelings while instrumentality is still kinda complete (the truth is painful but being ambiguous only causes more pain) perhaps he is trying to tell up to face reality and accept it no matter how much it sucks...this probably has to do with his depression during the making of the episodes...and maybe even the movie...

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Mon Jul 12, 2004 4:24 pm however..the question is not whether or not we are listening...it is whether or not we are willing to face reality...

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Mon Jul 12, 2004 7:03 pm We all have to face reality sooner or later. We can not keep running away from it. Even though we try to .

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Tue Jul 13, 2004 3:59 am thats what you think...there is always another path of avoidance...though it is unfavorable... i still wonder when i will face reality...

[]:             Quote Post by Somnius Kenate » Tue Jul 13, 2004 9:18 pm Trust me, after years of seeing a psychotherapist, hiding from reality is much more painful than facing the real world.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Thu Jul 15, 2004 1:46 pm its painful to hide as when faced by reality it hurts more...but...sometimes one can only find joy from hiding...and illusions... i guess i should stop hiding...

[]:             Quote Post by Somnius Kenate » Thu Jul 15, 2004 11:10 pm Look, from one guy on the internet to another, I know my opinion does not mean much. But if you find hope in a false reality, the short-term happiness you achieve will be just as false.

[]: Re: Existentialism and Eva...             Quote Post by Consul_Elect » Mon Jul 06, 2009 8:52 am Phaze wrote: I read a little bit on existentialism and noticed many interesting (though perhaps unintentional parallels) such as: Humanitys will to live, proof of mankinds existence, creating your own reality, and many other basic themes. It would be nice if somone who actually has some knowledge could go deeper into this, or just share their thoughts about what these existing parallels could mean.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Mon Jul 06, 2009 5:32 pm Anno apparently had an exitentialist-leaning therapist. The anime reflects his own process of realization. At the end, what else is there but to create a testament that you existed?

It would be nice if somone who actually has some knowledge could go deeper into this, or just share their thoughts about
